channel.title: Bayesian on Gabe's Gulch
channel.description: Recent content in Bayesian on Gabe's Gulch

item.title: Bayesian Updating: an Interactive Demo
item.description:
The Beta-Binomial model is the canonical conjugate prior example: given a coin with unknown bias $\theta$, we place a $\text{Beta}(\alpha, \beta)$ prior on it and observe $k$ heads in $n$ flips. Because the Beta is conjugate to the Binomial likelihood, the posterior is available in closed form as $\text{Beta}(\alpha + k,; \beta + n - k)$.

Use the sliders below to explore how the choice of prior and the amount of observed data shape the posterior — and watch the posterior mean interpolate between the prior mean and the maximum-likelihood estimate as $n$ grows.
